title: Tephrocactus sphaericus
intro: Tephrocactus sphaericus, also known as the globe prickly pear, is a species of cactus native to South America, characterized by its small, rounded bodies and bright green stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its high content of mucilages, flavonoids, and glycosides, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Tephrocactus sphaericus include flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as mucilages like mucilage and pectin. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Tephrocactus sphaericus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Opuntioideae, which comprises plants with flat, rounded or cylindrical stems. Within the genus Tephrocactus, T. sphaericus is one of several species of globular cacti. The plant is native to Mexico and is often found in areas with high elevations and dry conditions. T. sphaericus is a relatively rare species, but it has gained popularity among cactus enthusiasts due to its unique appearance and adaptability.
